news.text: Trump threatens Iran with power plant strikes over Hormuz oil blockade - Reuters
Saudi Arabia orders Iranian military attache, four embassy staff to leave - Reuters
Family of Iranian butcher missing in strike on commercial complex clings to hope - Reuters
3 themes that gripped Wall Street as stocks dropped for a 4th straight week
Trump threatens to deploy ICE agents to airports if DHS shutdown doesn't end, while Elon Musk offers to cover TSA agents' pay
Britain says Cyprus base will not be part of UK-US self-defence deal - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Futures bleeding red across the board with SPY down 1.70% to $648.57 as geopolitical tensions spike between US-Iran over Hormuz Strait oil blockade. VIX spiking +6.51% to 35.97 signals fear is back in play, while small caps getting hammered down 2.18% shows risk-off positioning ahead of what could be a volatile week.

◆ What to Watch

  • Trump's Iran Threat: Power plant strike threats escalating Middle East tensions - watch oil futures and defense stocks
  • DHS Shutdown Drama: Trump threatening ICE deployment at airports while Musk offers TSA pay coverage - infrastructure chaos potential
  • Saudi-Iran Diplomacy: Saudi expelling Iranian embassy staff signals broader regional destabilization
  • Fourth Week Decline: Market already in correction mode before this geopolitical flare-up
⚠ Bear Case
Hormuz Strait blockade could spike oil prices 20-30% overnight, crushing consumer spending just as economy shows cracks. VIX above 35 suggests more downside ahead.

◆ Sector Rotation

Flight to safety underway with only financials holding green (+0.18%) while defensive utilities getting crushed (-4.06%) - classic risk-off behavior. Tech leading declines at -2.27% with real estate also bleeding (-3.17%) as rate fears compound geopolitical stress. Energy barely red despite oil tensions, suggesting sector already priced for conflict.
⚡ Key Driver
Oil prices will dictate Monday's direction. Brent above $95 triggers energy rotation; below $85 suggests geopolitical premium fading fast.

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY $640: Critical support from February lows - break triggers capitulation selling
  • QQQ $575: 200-day moving average test - hold or die territory for tech bulls
  • VIX $40: Panic threshold that historically marks short-term bottoms
  • IWM $238: Small cap breakdown level - signals broader recession fears taking hold

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
